title: Getting started with cleanplots
text: want cleanplots colors – leaving theme, sizes, everything else exactly – add scale_color_cleanplots() (scale_fill_cleanplots()) plot, just like palette package:  scales take options: palette: "default" (main colors) "bars" (softer versions bars, areas, pies – ) order: use colors different order, e.g. order = c(7, 1, 2) starts navy reverse: reverse palette  use individual colors manually, cleanplots_colors() returns hex codes name:
code: ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width, color = Species)) +   geom_point() +   scale_color_cleanplots() ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width, color = Species)) +   geom_point() +   scale_color_cleanplots(order = c(7, 1, 2)) cleanplots_colors() #>       red    ltblue     black      gray    purple      pink      navy    ltgray  #> "#D50000" "#8FC6EB" "#000000" "#909090" "#740074" "#FFB3D9" "#143755" "#C0C0C0"  #>    dkgray  lavender  #> "#404040" "#D9D7F0" cleanplots_colors("red", "navy") #>       red      navy  #> "#D50000" "#143755"

title: Getting started with cleanplots
text: main palette used markers, lines, confidence intervals. alternates darker lighter colors first several groups remain distinguishable even printed black & white, contains red–green pair (common form colorblindness). bar palette contains softer versions colors. Bars, areas, pie slices use far ink points lines, full-strength colors overwhelm; Stata scheme automatically fades , package provides rendered colors:
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(class, fill = drv)) +   geom_bar(position = "dodge") +   scale_fill_cleanplots(palette = "bars")

title: Getting started with cleanplots
text: Color alone reliably distinguishes 4–5 groups. Beyond – black & white printing colorblind readers – vary marker shapes line patterns . scale_shape_cleanplots() scale_linetype_cleanplots() provide assignments Stata scheme, designed two groups sharing shape line pattern always differ strongly lightness: Shapes: hollow circle, solid circle, hollow square, solid square, hollow triangle, solid triangle, hollow diamond, solid diamond, plus, plus. Dark colors get hollow shapes; light colors get solid shapes. Line patterns: solid, solid, dashed, dashed, shortdash, shortdash, longdash, longdash, solid, solid.
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy, color = class, shape = class)) +   geom_point(size = 2, stroke = 0.7) +   scale_color_cleanplots() +   scale_shape_cleanplots() ggplot(economics_long, aes(date, value01, color = variable, linetype = variable)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.75) +   scale_color_cleanplots() +   scale_linetype_cleanplots()

title: Getting started with cleanplots
text: theme_cleanplots() applies scheme’s layout: white background, plot border, light gray axis lines, dotted gridlines, frameless legend lower right, black-outlined facet strips. Like ggplot2 theme, add per plot:
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy, color = class, shape = class)) +   geom_point(size = 2, stroke = 0.7) +   scale_color_cleanplots() +   scale_shape_cleanplots() +   theme_cleanplots()

title: Getting started with cleanplots
text: Adding scales themes every plot gets repetitive. One call top script makes whole session behave like Stata cleanplots scheme set: , scales theme added: every plot uses theme_cleanplots(); discrete color aesthetics use main palette, discrete fill aesthetics use softer bar palette (Stata, bars automatically get faded colors); points larger heavier outlines (size = 2, stroke = 0.7), hollow marker shapes clearly visible; lines thicker (linewidth = 0.75); error bars, linereanges, pointranges use 80% ; geom_smooth() fit lines cleanplots red light gray confidence band, instead ggplot2’s blue.   settings arguments prefer different sizes: Everything remains overridable per plot: explicit scale, theme, aesthetic always wins. example, use main palette fill instead automatic bar palette:  Undoing : cleanplots_defaults() sets session-wide state, persists restart R. reset manually:
code: cleanplots_defaults() ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy, color = class, shape = class)) +   geom_point() +   scale_shape_cleanplots() ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy)) +   geom_point(color = cleanplots_colors("gray")) +   geom_smooth(method = "loess", formula = y ~ x) cleanplots_defaults(base_size = 13, point_size = 2,                     point_stroke = 0.7, line_width = 0.75,                     smooth_color = "#D50000") ggplot(mpg, aes(class, fill = drv)) +   geom_bar(position = "dodge") +   scale_fill_cleanplots(palette = "default") theme_set(theme_gray()) options(ggplot2.discrete.colour = NULL, ggplot2.discrete.fill = NULL) update_geom_defaults("point", list(size = 1.5, stroke = 0.5))

what: 6. A note on plot windows vs. saved figures
title: Getting started with cleanplots
text: ggplot2 sizes (points, text, line widths) absolute physical units. maximized plot pane large monitor big canvas, elements look smaller saved figure. Judge sizes exported files (e.g., ggsave("fig.png", width = 8, height = 5.5)), ends papers slides – use camcorder package preview plots true export size.

title: Getting started with cleanplots
text: users moving R Stata, mapping : hex values languages computed definitions using Stata’s intensity-adjustment formulas, colors match exactly. Stata scheme available https://www.trentonmize.com/software/cleanplots.

what: cleanplots color scales and theme for ggplot2
title: cleanplots color scales and theme for ggplot2
text: Full documentation: tdmize.github.io/cleanplots Color scales theme ggplot2 implementing cleanplots graphing scheme Trenton D. Mize, originally developed Stata. scheme designed clean, publication-ready default plots — particularly plots predictions marginal effects.

title: cleanplots color scales and theme for ggplot2
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("tdmize/cleanplots")

title: cleanplots color scales and theme for ggplot2
text: quickest way use cleanplots one setup call, mirrors Stata scheme works: sets theme, makes markers lines larger thicker, applies cleanplots colors plots default (main palette color, softer bar palette fill): apply pieces individually per plot:
code: library(ggplot2) library(cleanplots) cleanplots_defaults()  ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy, color = class, shape = class)) +   geom_point() +   scale_shape_cleanplots() library(ggplot2) library(cleanplots)  # Scatterplot with the main palette and theme ggplot(iris, aes(x = Sepal.Length, y = Sepal.Width, color = Species)) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   theme_cleanplots() +   scale_color_cleanplots()  # Bar charts use the softer bar/area palette ggplot(mpg, aes(x = class, fill = drv)) +   geom_bar(position = "dodge") +   theme_cleanplots() +   scale_fill_cleanplots(palette = "bars")  # Reorder or reverse the colors scale_color_cleanplots(order = c(7, 1, 2)) scale_color_cleanplots(reverse = TRUE)  # Extract hex codes directly cleanplots_colors() cleanplots_colors("red", "navy") cleanplots_colors(bars = TRUE)

title: cleanplots color scales and theme for ggplot2
text: Main colors (palette = "default") — used markers, lines, confidence intervals Stata scheme: Bar/area colors (palette = "bars") — softer versions Stata scheme uses bar charts, area plots, pie charts, rendered scheme’s default intensity bar 70 (30% fade toward white):

title: cleanplots color scales and theme for ggplot2
text: theme_cleanplots() replicates non-color settings Stata scheme: white background, plot-region border, light gray axis lines ticks, dotted light gray gridlines directions, frameless, untitled legend lower right. restore legend title:
code: theme_cleanplots() + theme(legend.title = element_text())

title: cleanplots color scales and theme for ggplot2
text: Stata scheme also sets default marker symbols line patterns, make series distinguishable black & white colorblindness. Use scale_shape_cleanplots() scale_linetype_cleanplots() get behavior ggplot2:
code: # Markers: hollow shapes for dark colors, solid for light colors ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width,                  color = Species, shape = Species)) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   theme_cleanplots() +   scale_color_cleanplots() +   scale_shape_cleanplots()  # Line patterns: solid, solid, dashed, dashed, shortdash, shortdash, # longdash, longdash -- same-pattern series always differ in lightness ggplot(df, aes(x, y, color = group, linetype = group)) +   geom_line() +   theme_cleanplots() +   scale_color_cleanplots() +   scale_linetype_cleanplots()

title: cleanplots color scales and theme for ggplot2
text: palette alternates dark light colors first several series distinguishable lightness alone printed black & white, contains red-green pair, passes deuteranopia, protanopia, tritanopia simulation checks first five colors (minimum Delta-E 21). Beyond 4-5 series, add scale_shape_cleanplots() (points) scale_linetype_cleanplots() (lines): shapes patterns assigned two series sharing shape pattern always differ strongly lightness. Check palette Coloring Colorblindness.

title: cleanplots color scales and theme for ggplot2
text: original Stata scheme available https://www.trentonmize.com/software/cleanplots via net install cleanplots, ("https://tdmize.github.io/data/cleanplots") replace.

what: Extract cleanplots colors as hex codes — cleanplots_colors
title: Extract cleanplots colors as hex codes — cleanplots_colors
text: Get hex color codes used cleanplots color scheme, either full palette specific colors name.

title: Extract cleanplots colors as hex codes — cleanplots_colors
code: cleanplots_colors(..., bars = FALSE)

title: Extract cleanplots colors as hex codes — cleanplots_colors
text: ... Character names colors return. none specified, colors palette returned. Available names: "red", "ltblue", "black", "gray", "purple", "pink", "navy", "ltgray", "dkgray", "lavender". bars Logical. TRUE, return softer bar/area versions colors (colors Stata's cleanplots scheme uses bar charts, area plots, pie charts). Default FALSE (main colors used markers lines).

title: Extract cleanplots colors as hex codes — cleanplots_colors
text: named character vector hex color codes.

title: Extract cleanplots colors as hex codes — cleanplots_colors
text: Mize, T. D. cleanplots: Stata graphing scheme. https://www.trentonmize.com/software/cleanplots

title: Extract cleanplots colors as hex codes — cleanplots_colors
code: cleanplots_colors() #>       red    ltblue     black      gray    purple      pink      navy    ltgray  #> "#D50000" "#8FC6EB" "#000000" "#909090" "#740074" "#FFB3D9" "#143755" "#C0C0C0"  #>    dkgray  lavender  #> "#404040" "#D9D7F0"   cleanplots_colors("red", "navy") #>       red      navy  #> "#D50000" "#143755"   cleanplots_colors(bars = TRUE) #>       red    ltblue     black      gray    purple      pink      navy    ltgray  #> "#FF4D4D" "#C2E0F5" "#5F5F5F" "#B1B1B1" "#AF5FAF" "#FFDBEE" "#5D7A93" "#D3D3D3"  #>    dkgray  lavender  #> "#909090" "#E8E7F6"

what: Set cleanplots defaults for the session — cleanplots_defaults
title: Set cleanplots defaults for the session — cleanplots_defaults
text: One call makes ggplot2 behave like Stata cleanplots scheme rest session: sets theme_cleanplots() default theme, makes points, markers, lines larger thicker (matching scheme's visible defaults), registers cleanplots color fill scales session defaults plots colored correctly without adding scales hand.

title: Set cleanplots defaults for the session — cleanplots_defaults
code: cleanplots_defaults(   base_size = 13,   point_size = 2,   point_stroke = 0.7,   line_width = 0.75,   smooth_color = "#D50000" )

title: Set cleanplots defaults for the session — cleanplots_defaults
text: base_size Base font size points passed theme_cleanplots() (default: 13). point_size Default size geom_point() markers (default: 2; ggplot2's default 1.5). point_stroke Default outline thickness markers, controls visible hollow shapes (default: 0.7; ggplot2's default 0.5). line_width Default line width geom_line(), geom_path(), geom_step(), geom_smooth() (default: 0.75; ggplot2's default 0.5). Error bars, line ranges, point ranges set 80% value. smooth_color Default line color geom_smooth() (default: cleanplots red, "#D50000").

title: Set cleanplots defaults for the session — cleanplots_defaults
text: Invisibly returns NULL; called side effects.

title: Set cleanplots defaults for the session — cleanplots_defaults
text: calling cleanplots_defaults(): plots use theme_cleanplots() unless another theme added. geom_point() draws larger markers heavier outline (important hollow marker shapes). geom_line() friends draw thicker lines colors visible. geom_smooth() fit lines default cleanplots red light gray confidence band (instead ggplot2's blue). Discrete color aesthetics default main cleanplots palette, discrete fill aesthetics default softer bar palette (Stata, bars areas automatically use softer colors). Add scale explicitly override, e.g. scale_fill_cleanplots(palette = "default"). session-wide settings affect subsequent ggplot2 plots. undo , restart R call ggplot2::theme_set(ggplot2::theme_gray()), ggplot2::update_geom_defaults(), options(ggplot2.discrete.colour = NULL, ggplot2.discrete.fill = NULL).

title: Set cleanplots defaults for the session — cleanplots_defaults
code: library(ggplot2) cleanplots_defaults()  # No scales or theme needed -- everything is applied by default ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width, color = Species)) +   geom_point()

title: cleanplots color palette — palette_cleanplots
text: Generate palette function cleanplots color scheme. function usually called directly, within scale_color_cleanplots().

title: cleanplots color palette — palette_cleanplots
code: palette_cleanplots(palette = "default", reverse = FALSE, order = 1:10, ...)

title: cleanplots color palette — palette_cleanplots
text: palette Character name palette. One "default" (main colors used markers, lines, confidence intervals Stata cleanplots scheme) "bars" (softer, lighter versions scheme uses bar charts, area plots, pie charts). reverse Logical. palette reversed? order vector numbers 1 10 indicating order colors use (default: 1:10). ... Ignored (included compatibility).

title: cleanplots color palette — palette_cleanplots
text: function takes single integer n returns character vector n hex colors.

title: cleanplots color palette — palette_cleanplots
code: palette_cleanplots()(4) #> [1] "#D50000" "#8FC6EB" "#000000" "#909090"  palette_cleanplots("bars")(3) #> [1] "#FF4D4D" "#C2E0F5" "#5F5F5F"

what: cleanplots color scales for ggplot2 — scale_color_cleanplots
title: cleanplots color scales for ggplot2 — scale_color_cleanplots
text: Discrete color fill scales using cleanplots color scheme (Mize), port colors Stata cleanplots graphing scheme. palette designed clean, publication-ready plots predictions marginal effects.

title: cleanplots color scales for ggplot2 — scale_color_cleanplots
code: scale_color_cleanplots(   palette = "default",   reverse = FALSE,   order = 1:10,   aesthetics = "color",   ... )  scale_colour_cleanplots(   palette = "default",   reverse = FALSE,   order = 1:10,   aesthetics = "color",   ... )  scale_fill_cleanplots(   palette = "default",   reverse = FALSE,   order = 1:10,   aesthetics = "fill",   ... )

title: cleanplots color scales for ggplot2 — scale_color_cleanplots
text: palette Character name palette: "default" "bars". reverse Logical. palette reversed? order vector numbers 1 10 indicating order colors use (default: 1:10). aesthetics vector aesthetic names scale apply (e.g., "color" "fill"). ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::discrete_scale().

title: cleanplots color scales for ggplot2 — scale_color_cleanplots
text: "default" palette contains 10 colors used markers, lines, confidence intervals. "bars" palette contains softer, lighter versions colors Stata scheme uses bar charts, area plots, pie charts (elements use much ink); typically paired scale_fill_cleanplots(). cleanplots palette available discrete palette.

title: cleanplots color scales for ggplot2 — scale_color_cleanplots
text: Mize, T. D. cleanplots: Stata graphing scheme. https://www.trentonmize.com/software/cleanplots

title: cleanplots color scales for ggplot2 — scale_color_cleanplots
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(iris, aes(x = Sepal.Length, y = Sepal.Width, color = Species)) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   theme_cleanplots() +   scale_color_cleanplots()   ggplot(iris, aes(x = Species, y = Sepal.Length, fill = Species)) +   geom_boxplot() +   theme_cleanplots() +   scale_fill_cleanplots(palette = "bars")   # Use colors in a different order ggplot(iris, aes(x = Sepal.Length, y = Sepal.Width, color = Species)) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   theme_cleanplots() +   scale_color_cleanplots(order = c(7, 1, 2))

what: cleanplots shape and linetype scales for ggplot2 — scale_shape_cleanplots
title: cleanplots shape and linetype scales for ggplot2 — scale_shape_cleanplots
text: Discrete shape linetype scales matching marker symbols line patterns Stata cleanplots scheme. Combined scale_color_cleanplots(), make series distinguishable even printed black & white viewed colorblindness: markers alternate hollow (dark colors) solid (light colors) shapes, series share line pattern always differ strongly lightness.

title: cleanplots shape and linetype scales for ggplot2 — scale_shape_cleanplots
code: scale_shape_cleanplots(...)  scale_linetype_cleanplots(...)

title: cleanplots shape and linetype scales for ggplot2 — scale_shape_cleanplots
text: ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::scale_shape_manual() ggplot2::scale_linetype_manual().

title: cleanplots shape and linetype scales for ggplot2 — scale_shape_cleanplots
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width,                  color = Species, shape = Species)) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   theme_cleanplots() +   scale_color_cleanplots() +   scale_shape_cleanplots()   ggplot(economics_long, aes(date, value01,                            color = variable, linetype = variable)) +   geom_line() +   theme_cleanplots() +   scale_color_cleanplots() +   scale_linetype_cleanplots()

title: cleanplots theme for ggplot2 — theme_cleanplots
text: ggplot2 theme replicates non-color settings Stata cleanplots graphing scheme: white background, plot-region border, light gray (gs10) axis lines ticks, dotted light gray horizontal vertical gridlines, frameless legend lower right.

title: cleanplots theme for ggplot2 — theme_cleanplots
code: theme_cleanplots(base_size = 13, base_family = "", legend_position = "right")

title: cleanplots theme for ggplot2 — theme_cleanplots
text: base_size Base font size points (default: 13). base_family Base font family (default: ""). legend_position Position legend (default: "right", bottom-justified, matching Stata scheme's 4-o'clock position). Set "none" remove, value accepted ggplot2::theme(legend.position = ...).

title: cleanplots theme for ggplot2 — theme_cleanplots
text: ggplot2 theme object.

title: cleanplots theme for ggplot2 — theme_cleanplots
text: Correspondence Stata scheme file: White background (color background white) plot region border (linestyle plotregion none) Axis lines, ticks gs10 gray (color axisline gs10, etc.) Dotted gs10 major gridlines, horizontal vertical (draw_major_hgrid yes, draw_major_vgrid yes, linepattern major_grid dot, color major_grid gs10) minor gridlines Legend lower right, single column, frame, title (clockdir legend_position 4, numstyle legend_cols 1, linestyle legend none) Horizontal y-axis tick labels (anglestyle vertical_tick horizontal) Facet strips: black-outlined, unfilled boxes bold labels

title: cleanplots theme for ggplot2 — theme_cleanplots
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(iris, aes(x = Sepal.Length, y = Sepal.Width, color = Species)) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   theme_cleanplots() +   scale_color_cleanplots()

title: cleanplots 0.1.0
text: Initial release. cleanplots_defaults(): one-call session setup – default theme, larger markers thicker lines, cleanplots colors applied plots automatically, geom_smooth() fit lines cleanplots red. theme_cleanplots(): facet strips drawn black-outlined, unfilled boxes bold labels. Discrete color fill scales (scale_color_cleanplots(), scale_fill_cleanplots()) main bar/area palettes. Shape linetype scales (scale_shape_cleanplots(), scale_linetype_cleanplots()) matching Stata scheme’s marker symbols line patterns. theme_cleanplots() replicating layout defaults Stata cleanplots graphing scheme (v1.0.4). cleanplots_colors() palette_cleanplots() extract hex codes.
